items.front: What is the traffic management mission, and what tool is preferred when feasible?
items.back: Balance air traffic demand with system capacity for the most efficient use of the NAS. When feasible, use TBFM in preference to miles-in-trail initiatives, and remember controllers are part of that mission.

items.front: What is the mission of the traffic management system?
items.back: Balance air traffic demand with system capacity to ensure maximum efficient utilization of the NAS. TBFM must be used in preference to miles-in-trail. Per 11-1-1(a-b).

items.front: What must the STMCIC ensure during day and evening shifts?
items.back: Ensure a day or evening operational briefing happens with the required participants and key operational topics, and ensure TBM operations and TMIs are implemented and updated promptly.

items.front: During metering, what are the basic TBFM compliance responsibilities?
items.back: Display TBFM schedule information, meet generated metering times within plus or minus 1 minute, use MIT or MINIT for specific unstable aircraft if needed, and coordinate when you cannot comply.

items.front: What is the TBFM metering tolerance?
items.back: Plus or minus 1 minute. If accuracy cannot be used, apply MIT or MINIT until stability resumes. Per 11-1-3(b)(1).
